derivation_notes.levid_location: VALUE field (8-byte LE uint64) of each index entry
derivation_notes.sort_semantics: id bytes compared by memcmp (32-byte hex id, sorted as raw bytes); created_at compared NUMERICALLY. Since each event has a unique id, the created_at never affects ordering — the id alone determines order.

adversarial_demonstration.note: Each event has a unique id. The adversarial created_at pair (ts=1700000255 vs ts=1700000256) appears in different id-prefix positions — demonstrating the golpe StringUint64 sort correctly handles the LE byte order for created_at even when mixed with arbitrary id prefixes.
adversarial_demonstration.example: levId=5 (id=4d401c51..., ts=1700000255) and levId=7 (id=dbcded37..., ts=1700000256) sort by id first (4d < db), so created_at is not the tiebreaker here — but the LE inversion is present in the key layout and handled by the comparator.
